source_pr.title: parser, core: Implement force_index hint in parser and TiDB
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: 
Fix #15519 

Before:
```
TiDB(root@127.0.0.1:test) > select /*+ force_index(aaa) */ * from t;
Empty set, 1 warning (0.00 sec)

TiDB(root@127.0.0.1:test) > show warnings;
+---------+------+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Level   | Code | Message                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
+---------+------+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Warning | 1064 | You have an error in your SQL syntax; check the manual that corresponds to your TiDB version for the right syntax to use [parser:8064]Optimizer hint syntax error at line 1 column 22 near "force_index(aaa) */"  |
+---------+------+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)
```

After:
```
mysql> select /*+ force_index(aaa) */ * from t;
Empty set, 1 warning (0.00 sec)

mysql> show warnings;
+---------+------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Level   | Code | Message                                                                         |
+---------+------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Warning | 1815 | force_index(test.aaa) is inapplicable, check whether the table(test.aaa) exists |
+---------+------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)
```

Problem Summary: 
The parser does not support force_index optimizer hint.

### What is changed and how it works?

What's Changed:
1. implement force_index hint in TiDB parser
2. handle force_index in pushTableHints

How it Works:

- Implement force_index in parser first. 
- The logic for processing force_index is the same as FORCE INDEX, so there is no need to implement additional logic. 
- In TiDB, force_index and use_index have the same processing logic, force_index uses the same test example as use_index.

### Related changes

- pingcap/parser https://github.com/pingcap/parser/pull/1205

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- Integration test

Side effects

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- Implement force_index hint in parser and TiDB

symptoms: tk.MustQuery("select /*+ FORCE_INDEX(t, i_a), USE_INDEX(t, i_b) */ a from t order by a") (planner/core/integration_test.go)
failure_signature: tk.MustQuery("select /*+ FORCE_INDEX(t, i_a), USE_INDEX(t, i_b) */ a from t order by a") (planner/core/integration_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it asserted an exact plan/EXPLAIN output that can vary between runs. Exact plan/EXPLAIN assertions are brittle when optimizer choices can vary. This PR stabilizes it by: tk.MustQuery("select /*+ FORCE_INDEX(t, i_a), USE_INDEX(t, i_b) */ a from t order by a") (planner/core/integration_test.go).
fix_pattern: tk.MustQuery("select /*+ FORCE_INDEX(t, i_a), USE_INDEX(t, i_b) */ a from t order by a") (planner/core/integration_test.go)

evidence: evidence: tk.MustQuery("select /*+ FORCE_INDEX(t, i_a), USE_INDEX(t, i_b) */ a from t order by a") (planner/core/integration_test.go)

files: go.mod, go.sum, planner/core/integration_test.go, planner/core/logical_plan_builder.go, planner/core/logical_plan_test.go, planner/core/testdata/integration_suite_out.json, planner/core/testdata/plan_suite_in.json, planner/core/testdata/plan_suite_out.json
